# OELTKit — Open E-Learning Toolkit (LLM guide)

OELTKit turns a single course tree into SCORM 1.2 / SCORM 2004 / cmi5 / standalone-web
packages. You (the LLM) author a `course.json` manifest plus free-HTML pages; the toolkit
handles tracking, accessibility plumbing, and packaging. Write bespoke HTML freely — only the
chrome (tracking, navigation, a11y, packaging) is standardized.

This file is self-contained: everything needed to scaffold, author, validate, and package a
course. Naming: npm packages are `@oeltkit/*`; everything author-facing is `oelt`
(CLI `oelt`, elements `<oelt-*>`, runtime global `oelt`, CSS tokens `--oelt-*`).

## Workflow

```bash
oelt new my-course                       # scaffold course.json + pages/p1.html
# …edit course.json and pages/*.html…
oelt validate my-course                  # schema + a11y + tracking checks (--json for machine output)
oelt preview my-course                   # local fake-LMS harness with a live tracking panel
oelt package my-course --target scorm12  # → my-course-id-scorm12.zip  (also: scorm2004 | cmi5 | web)
oelt export my-course                    # → my-course.oeltcourse (single-file for transport/MCP)
oelt import my-course.oeltcourse dest/   # extract .oeltcourse to a directory
```

`oelt package` refuses to build if `oelt validate` finds errors. Fix them first.

`validate`, `package`, and `preview` also accept a `.oeltcourse` file directly — they extract
it to a temp directory, run the command, and clean up.

### .oeltcourse (single-file format)

A `.oeltcourse` file is a ZIP archive containing the standard course tree (`course.json` at the
root plus all page and asset files). It is the exchange format between authoring tools, LLM
clients, and CI pipelines. Round-trippable: `export → import → identical tree`.

- Use `oelt export <dir>` to create one; `oelt import <file> <dir>` to extract.
- Consumers (MCP server, oeltkit-cloud) call `oelt import` rather than reimplementing extraction
  so zip-slip fixes propagate automatically.

## course.json (the manifest)

One JSON document is the source of truth. Model: structured shell + free-HTML body. Pages are
HTML files referenced by `src`; a page MAY declare its tracked `interactions` so tracking is
machine-checkable without parsing HTML.

```json
{
  "oelt": "0.1",
  "id": "com.example.data-privacy",
  "title": "Data Privacy Essentials",
  "lang": "en",
  "targets": ["scorm12", "scorm2004", "cmi5", "web"],
  "theme": "./theme/tokens.css",
  "tracking": {
    "completion": { "rule": "required-interactions-passed" },
    "score": { "rule": "weighted-interactions", "mastery": 0.8 },
    "progress": { "rule": "pages-viewed" }
  },
  "structure": [
    {
      "id": "m1",
      "title": "Foundations",
      "pages": [
        { "id": "intro", "title": "Why privacy matters", "src": "pages/intro.html" },
        {
          "id": "quiz",
          "title": "Final quiz",
          "src": "pages/quiz.html",
          "interactions": [{ "id": "final-quiz", "type": "quiz", "weight": 1, "required": true }]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Rules:

- `id`: reverse-DNS, lowercase, e.g. `com.example.course-name`. All module/page/interaction ids
  must be unique across the whole course and be valid HTML id tokens (`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$`).
- `lang`: BCP-47 (required — drives the document language for accessibility).
- `targets`: any non-empty subset of `scorm12`, `scorm2004`, `cmi5`, `web`.
- `tracking` is optional. Omit it for the zero-config default: complete when all pages are viewed,
  no score. Each interaction's `id` MUST match the `id` of an element in that page's HTML.
- Unknown keys are rejected — no typos.

### Tracking vocabulary (closed set)

completion `rule`:

- `all-pages-viewed` (default)
- `pages-viewed` — requires `threshold` (0–1), e.g. view 80% of pages
- `required-interactions-completed` — every `required: true` interaction answered
- `required-interactions-passed` — every `required: true` interaction passed
- `manual` — author code calls `oelt.track.complete()`

score `rule`:

- `none` (default)
- `single-interaction` — requires `source` (an interaction id)
- `weighted-interactions` — weighted mean using each interaction's `weight`
- `mastery` (0–1) may be set on a scoring rule to decide pass/fail.

progress `rule`: `pages-viewed` (default) | `none`.

**SCORM 1.2 collapse rule:** SCORM 1.2 has one status field. If a score rule + `mastery` are
defined, the course reports `passed`/`failed`; otherwise `completed`/`incomplete`. (SCORM 2004 and
cmi5 report completion and success separately.) You don't implement this — the runtime does.

## Authoring pages

A page (`src`) is a free HTML fragment (no `<html>`/`<body>` wrapper — just the content). Use any
HTML/CSS/SVG/JS. Use OELT components for tracked, accessible interactions. Keep one `<h1>` per page.

### Components (`<oelt-*>`)

The runtime auto-wires component interactions to tracking — you write no tracking code in markup.

**Multiple choice / multiple response — `<oelt-mcq>`**

```html
<oelt-mcq id="final-quiz" mode="single" key="b">
  <p slot="prompt">Which standard is recommended for new content?</p>
  <oelt-option value="a">SCORM 1.2 only</oelt-option>
  <oelt-option value="b">cmi5</oelt-option>
  <oelt-option value="c">AICC</oelt-option>
  <p slot="correct">Correct — cmi5 is the modern target.</p>
  <p slot="incorrect">Not quite — cmi5 is recommended for new content.</p>
</oelt-mcq>
```

- `mode="multiple"` for checkboxes; `key` then lists every correct value (space-separated).
- `manual-grade` (omit `key`) for ungraded polls.
- The element `id` must equal the manifest interaction id.

**Branching scenario — `<oelt-branching>`**

```html
<oelt-branching id="scenario1" start="n1">
  <script type="application/json">
    {
      "nodes": {
        "n1": {
          "text": "<p>A colleague asks for your password. You…</p>",
          "choices": [
            { "label": "Share it", "to": "bad", "value": "share" },
            { "label": "Refuse and report it", "to": "good", "value": "refuse" }
          ]
        },
        "good": { "text": "<p>Correct call.</p>", "end": "passed" },
        "bad": { "text": "<p>That exposes credentials.</p>", "choices": [{ "label": "Back", "to": "n1", "value": "back" }] }
      }
    }
  </script>
</oelt-branching>
```

A node with `end` (`passed`/`failed`/`completed`) is terminal. Or load the graph with
`src="scenarios/x.json"` instead of the inline `<script>`.

**Media — `<oelt-media>`**

```html
<oelt-media id="intro-video" threshold="0.9">
  <video controls preload="metadata" width="480">
    <source src="media/intro.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
    <track kind="captions" src="media/intro.en.vtt" srclang="en" label="English" default />
  </video>
  <div slot="transcript"><p>Full transcript…</p></div>
</oelt-media>
```

REQUIRED: video needs a captions `<track>` or a `transcript` slot; audio needs a `transcript`
slot. Without one, the component refuses to render and `oelt validate` fails.

### Bespoke interactions

For a one-off interaction, emit the same event the components use; the runtime forwards it:

```html
<button onclick="oelt.track.interaction({ id: 'final-quiz', type: 'quiz', result: 'passed', score: 1 })">
  Submit
</button>
```

`result` ∈ `passed` | `failed` | `completed`; `score` is 0–1.

## Runtime API (global `oelt`, available after the page loads)

- `oelt.track.complete()` — mark complete (with the `manual` rule).
- `oelt.track.score(0.85)` — report a 0–1 score.
- `oelt.track.progress(0.4)` — report 0–1 progress.
- `oelt.track.interaction({ id, type, result, score?, response? })` — report an interaction.
- `oelt.state.get(key)` / `oelt.state.set(key, value)` — suspend/resume key-value store. Budget
  3 KB total; oversized writes throw. Keys starting with `__` are reserved.
- `oelt.nav.pages`, `oelt.nav.current()`, `oelt.nav.go(i)`, `oelt.nav.next()`, `oelt.nav.prev()`.

Identical content runs on every target; the runtime auto-detects the LMS at launch.

## Accessibility (enforced)

- Use native elements (`<button>`, `<input>`, `<fieldset>`/`<legend>`); avoid hand-rolled ARIA.
- Every page needs a logical heading order starting at `<h1>`.
- Media must have captions or a transcript (validator-enforced).
- Don't convey meaning by color alone; ensure keyboard operability and visible focus.
- Components are accessible by construction; bespoke content is your responsibility — `oelt validate`
  and the components help, but follow these rules.

## Validate & package

`oelt validate <dir|file.oeltcourse> [--json]` checks: schema, id uniqueness, declared interactions
exist in the page HTML, media has captions/transcript, and tracking is reachable (e.g. a
`required-interactions-*` rule has a required interaction; a `single-interaction` score names a real
interaction).

With `--json`, each finding includes `code` (machine-stable, for programmatic matching), `message`
(terse technical description), and `message_human` (plain-language action sentence naming pages by
title — suitable for displaying directly to the author or feeding back to an LLM for auto-fix).

`oelt package <dir> --target <scorm12|scorm2004|cmi5|web> [--out file.zip]` generates the manifest
(`imsmanifest.xml` / `cmi5.xml`) and a self-contained zip. Never hand-edit those manifests — they
are generated from `course.json`.

## Complete minimal example

`course.json`:

```json
{
  "oelt": "0.1",
  "id": "com.example.hello",
  "title": "Hello OELT",
  "lang": "en",
  "targets": ["scorm12", "web"],
  "tracking": { "completion": { "rule": "required-interactions-passed" }, "score": { "rule": "single-interaction", "source": "q1", "mastery": 0.8 } },
  "structure": [
    { "id": "m1", "title": "Module 1", "pages": [
      { "id": "intro", "title": "Intro", "src": "pages/intro.html" },
      { "id": "check", "title": "Check", "src": "pages/check.html", "interactions": [{ "id": "q1", "type": "choice", "required": true }] }
    ] }
  ]
}
```

`pages/intro.html`:

```html
<section><h1>Intro</h1><p>Welcome.</p></section>
```

`pages/check.html`:

```html
<section>
  <h1>Check</h1>
  <oelt-mcq id="q1" mode="single" key="b">
    <p slot="prompt">Is OELT tracking per-target?</p>
    <oelt-option value="a">Yes</oelt-option>
    <oelt-option value="b">No — one model, four targets</oelt-option>
  </oelt-mcq>
</section>
```

Then: `oelt validate . && oelt package . --target scorm12`.
